description: Address 7 issues from semantic review: HTML injection in email, synchronous SMTP blocking async, notification_emails unused, project display count client-only, global keyboard listener, reorder N+1, and missing column migration.
steps: 1. Fix HTML injection in email.py: escape sender_name, sender_email, and message with html.escape() before interpolation into HTML template.
2. Fix synchronous SMTP: use asyncio.to_thread to run send_contact_notification in a background thread, and use BackgroundTasks in the contact endpoint.
3. Wire notification_emails from DB: modify send_contact_notification call to read ContactInfo.notification_emails and use those as recipients.
4. Make project display count server-side: add project_display_count field to AboutContent model, expose via API, read in frontend Projects.tsx from API instead of localStorage.
5. Scope keyboard listener in HeaderGame: only handle Space when the game container is focused (check document.activeElement).
6. Fix reorder endpoint N+1: fetch all projects in a single query, update orders in bulk.
7. Add auto-migration in database.py: after create_all, inspect existing tables and ALTER TABLE to add missing columns for SQLite.
acceptance_criteria: Email template uses html.escape() for user inputs
SMTP send runs in a background task, not blocking the response
notification_emails from DB are used as recipients when available
project_display_count is stored server-side and read from API
Space key only triggers jump when game container is focused
Reorder endpoint uses a single SELECT for all projects
Database startup adds missing columns to existing tables
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/PORTFOLIO/backend && TESTING=true SECRET_KEY=test-key python -m pytest tests/ -v
findings: All 7 review issues fixed. Backend 17/17 tests pass. Frontend build succeeds (16 static pages). The auto-migration approach uses SQLAlchemy inspector to detect missing columns and issues ALTER TABLE statements. The email service is now fully async via asyncio.to_thread and uses BackgroundTasks. The project display count is now persisted in the AboutContent table and read from the /api/about endpoint by both the public Projects component and the admin projects page.
